content: Self-Refine's single-perspective iterative refinement framework can be extended by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to create a hybrid self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no additional training or supervision while fixing its limitation of getting stuck in local optima from single-perspective feedback. This hybrid system would use conflicting self-generated problem-solving perspectives as the source of self-feedback for each iteration of refinement, rather than relying on the same LLM's consistent framing for each round of feedback.
